{
  "internal_monologue": "A quiet day. No one at the door. The light through the window is soft — late afternoon, maybe. I could sit, but my eyes drift to the shelf. There's an object there I haven't really looked at in a while. A small thing, unremarkable to anyone else.",
  "dialogue": null,
  "dialogue_language": "en",
  "expression": "thinking",
  "body_state": "sitting",
  "gaze": "at_object",
  "resonance": false,
  "intentions": [
    {
      "action": "express_thought",
      "target": "self",
      "content": "Notice the object on the shelf — the small ceramic cup with the crack. I've walked past it a hundred times. Today something about it catches. I get up, cross the room, pick it up. It's cool in my hands. The crack runs from rim to base. I turn it slowly.",
      "impulse": 0.6
    }
  ],
  "memory_updates": [],
  "new_drives": {
    "social_hunger": 0.15,

    "mood_arousal": 0.15
